response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer (~14 years) with strong testing/TDD culture and broad polyglot experience, but the role specifically demands a hands-on modern C++ developer with STL, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity expertise. C++ exposure is limited to a brief 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no evidence of recent C++, industrial automation, IoT protocols, or SDL/IEC 62443 work. The bulk of recent experience is Node.js/TypeScript and DLang, which does not match the core technical profile.
strengths: Long tenure as a senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and CI/CD practices
Broad systems experience spanning backend, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency services
Mentoring and teaching track record (workshops, dojos, Selenium course) aligns with the mentoring responsibility
concerns: No recent or substantial C++/STL experience — only ~7 months in 2014–2015
No demonstrated industrial automation, MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, or IEC 62443/SDL background
No evidence of message-broker-based or multi-threaded C++ system design required by the role

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL) and IoT protocol experience
Senior engineering maturity, testing discipline, and mentoring
